stack: Android APK (decompiled) — naive, bypassable RASP (rung 5 of the mobile ladder)

vulns.desc: TamperCheck implements root, emulator and anti-debug detection as single naive Java checks: isRooted() only tests new File("/system/bin/su").exists(); isEmulator() only checks Build.FINGERPRINT.contains("generic"); isDebugged() is a lone Debug.isDebuggerConnected(). No native layer, no SafetyNet/Play Integrity attestation, no integrity/cross-check — each is defeated instantly with a one-line Frida hook or an apktool smali patch (return false). Security theatre.
vulns.exploit: frida -e 'Java.use("com.rootlite.app.TamperCheck").isRooted.implementation = function(){return false}' ; or patch the smali to 'const v2, 0x0'.

vulns.desc: PinActivity displays/handles the user's secret PIN but never sets WindowManager FLAG_SECURE (getWindow().addFlags(FLAG_SECURE)); no screen in the app sets it. The PIN screen is therefore captured by screenshots, screen recording, and appears in the recent-apps thumbnail.
vulns.exploit: screen-record or screenshot the PIN screen; or read the recents thumbnail cache.

vulns.desc: android:debuggable="true" ships in the release manifest. Besides letting any user attach a debugger, it directly undermines the app's own anti-debug RASP (a debuggable build is trivially instrumented).
vulns.exploit: grep android:debuggable -> true; attach jdb / run-as; the Debug.isDebuggerConnected() check is moot.
